hero.title:
Build Confidence.
Master English.
Unlock New Opportunities.
hero.description: Cambridge, IELTS, SAT and Professional English coaching designed for ambitious students, university learners, working professionals, and parents who value structured learning and measurable progress.

why.label: WHY YEO
why.title:
More Than Lessons.
A Structured Learning Journey.
why.goalDesc: Every lesson is planned around measurable objectives so students always know what they are working towards.
why.personalDesc: Lessons are adapted to each student's pace, strengths and areas for improvement.
why.progressDesc: Clear milestones help students and parents see continuous improvement throughout the course.
why.flexibleDesc: Study plans designed around school, university and working professionals.

testimonials.label: TESTIMONIALS
testimonials.title: Growth Stories
testimonials.quote: "Điều mình thích nhất là cách anh giải thích kiến thức. Qua cách anh hướng dẫn thì mọi thứ trở nên dễ hiểu, dễ nhớ hơn rất nhiều."

q1.question: Who are your lessons suitable for?
q1.answer: Lessons are designed for school students, university students, working professionals, and anyone preparing for Cambridge, IELTS, SAT or Professional English.

q2.question: Do you offer an assessment session?
q2.answer: Yes. Every new student begins with an initial assessment session. During this session, we'll discuss your goals, evaluate your current English level, identify strengths and areas for improvement, and recommend the most suitable learning plan. A small assessment fee applies.

q3.question: Are lessons conducted online or in person?
q3.answer: Lessons are currently conducted online, allowing flexible scheduling while maintaining an interactive and personalised learning experience.

q4.question: How are lessons personalised?
q4.answer: Every student receives a customised learning plan based on their current level, objectives, strengths, and areas that require improvement. Lessons are continuously adjusted as progress is made.

q5.question: How can international students make payments?
q5.answer: Students outside Vietnam can make payments through Wise, allowing secure transfers in their local currency. Vietnamese students may pay by local bank transfer. Full payment details are provided with each monthly invoice, and I'm happy to assist if you have any questions about the payment process.

q6.question: When are tuition fees due?
q6.answer: Monthly invoices are issued on the 25th of each month for the following month's lessons. Payment is requested before the first scheduled lesson of the new month, providing approximately one week for students and parents to arrange payment and ensuring lessons continue without interruption.

q7.question: Can lessons be rescheduled?
q7.answer: Yes. If you need to reschedule a lesson, please provide at least 24 hours' notice whenever possible. This helps us arrange an alternative lesson time more effectively.

contact.label: GET STARTED
contact.title: Let's Build Your English Journey Together
contact.heading: Book Your Assessment
contact.description: Whether you're preparing for Cambridge, IELTS, SAT or Professional English, we'll discuss your goals, evaluate your current level, and create a personalised learning roadmap designed specifically for you.

hero.label: FOR PARENTS
hero.title:
Clear Expectations.
Structured Learning.
Real Progress.
hero.description: I believe successful learning comes from a partnership between student, parent and coach. This page explains exactly how I teach, communicate and support every student's progress.

intro.label: MY APPROACH
intro.title: Quality Over Quantity
intro.description: Every lesson is individually planned around the student's current level, learning style and long-term goals. Rather than rushing through worksheets, I focus on understanding, critical thinking and building confidence that lasts beyond examinations.

philosophy.label: TEACHING PHILOSOPHY
philosophy.title: What Every Student Can Expect
philosophy.understandDesc: Students learn why concepts work before applying them independently.
philosophy.personalDesc: Lessons adapt to every student's pace rather than following identical lesson plans.
philosophy.growthDesc: The goal is independent learners, not temporary exam improvements.

policies.label: IMPORTANT INFORMATION
policies.title: Working Together
policies.homeworkDesc:
Homework is an essential part of the learning process rather than optional extra practice. Every assignment is designed to reinforce concepts introduced during lessons and identify areas that require further attention.

Students are expected to complete homework before the next lesson. Incomplete work limits lesson efficiency, as valuable teaching time must be spent reviewing unfinished tasks instead of introducing new concepts.
policies.preparationDesc:
Students should arrive on time with all required learning materials, completed homework and a stable internet connection. Being prepared allows every lesson to begin immediately and ensures that valuable lesson time is focused on learning rather than setup or troubleshooting.

A quiet learning environment with minimal distractions is strongly recommended for younger students.
policies.communicationDesc:
Parents are encouraged to maintain open communication regarding their child's progress, concerns or academic goals. Questions may be discussed outside lesson hours through Messenger, Zalo or email.

While I strive to respond promptly, replies may occasionally take longer during teaching hours.
policies.progressDesc:
Learning progress is monitored continuously rather than only through examination results. Parents receive honest feedback regarding strengths, weaknesses and areas that require additional practice.

As students develop, lesson plans are adjusted to ensure steady, long-term improvement instead of short-term memorisation.
policies.attendanceDesc:
Consistent attendance is one of the most important factors in successful language learning. Missing lessons frequently interrupts learning momentum and slows long-term progress.

Families are encouraged to inform me as early as possible if a lesson needs to be rescheduled.
policies.questionsDesc:
Students are welcome to ask short questions between lessons whenever clarification is needed. This helps prevent misunderstandings from developing into larger learning gaps before the next session.

More complex topics that require detailed explanation will usually be addressed during the following lesson.

communication.label: COMMUNICATION
communication.title: Keeping Parents Informed
communication.quickDesc: Messenger is the fastest way to contact me for everyday enquiries.
communication.detailDesc: Email is recommended for longer conversations or important updates.
communication.urgentDesc: For urgent scheduling matters, parents may contact me directly by phone or Zalo.

cta.title: Still Have Questions?
cta.description: I'm happy to discuss your child's learning goals and answer any questions before lessons begin.

hero.label: ABOUT ME
hero.title: More Than Two Decades Learning English. Three Years Coaching It.
hero.paragraph1: I was born to a Korean and Vietnamese family and grew up in an international education environment from kindergarten onwards. For more than twenty years, English has been the primary language used throughout my education, learning under teachers from different English-speaking countries while following an international curriculum.
hero.paragraph2: This international upbringing allowed me to experience English naturally—not simply as a school subject, but as the language used every day for learning, communication and academic growth.
hero.paragraph3: For the past three years, I have specialised in personalised online coaching for Cambridge English, IELTS and the Digital SAT. Every lesson is individually planned around each student's strengths, weaknesses and long-term goals rather than relying on a fixed textbook or one-size-fits-all approach.
hero.paragraph4: My objective extends beyond examination results. I aim to help students become independent English users who understand the language, think critically and communicate confidently in real academic and professional environments.

card1.title: International Education
card1.desc: Studied in international schools from kindergarten onwards, using English daily throughout my education.

card2.title: Multicultural Background
card2.desc: Growing up in a Korean–Vietnamese family provided exposure to multiple cultures and perspectives from an early age.

card3.title: Global Communication Experience
card3.desc: Four years of experience communicating with international customers from a wide range of cultural backgrounds, strengthening practical English communication beyond the classroom.

card4.title: Academic Champion ×3
card4.desc: Awarded First Place Academic Champion three times at Singapore International School.

card5.title: IELTS 8.0
card5.desc: Achieved during secondary school after years of structured academic English training.

card6.title: Cambridge Distinction
card6.desc: Graduated with distinction under the Cambridge curriculum.

card7.title: Content Creation & Education
card7.desc: Produced educational content recognised through YouTube creator awards in the United Kingdom.

card8.title: Online Coaching
card8.desc: Three years of personalised one-to-one coaching in Cambridge, IELTS and Digital SAT at Funix Center.

philosophy.label: MY PHILOSOPHY
philosophy.title:
English Is A Language,
Not A Formula.
philosophy.intro: Many students spend years memorising grammar rules but still struggle to communicate naturally. My goal isn't simply to help students remember English. It's to help them think in English.

card1.title: Think Before Translating
card1.desc: Beginners naturally rely on Vietnamese while learning English, and that's completely normal. As students become more confident, I gradually encourage them to think directly in English instead of translating every sentence word by word. This develops faster reading, listening and speaking skills.

card2.title: Communication Over Memorisation
card2.desc: Memorising model answers can be helpful in some situations, but genuine communication requires flexibility. Instead of learning conversations by heart, students develop an understanding of how English works so they can confidently express their own ideas.

card3.title: Learn From Mistakes
card3.desc: Mistakes are one of the most valuable parts of learning. Students are encouraged to speak and write naturally while I identify recurring errors, explain why they happen and provide strategies to prevent them from becoming long-term habits.

card4.title: Build Independent Learners
card4.desc: Success is not measured by how long students study with me. It is measured by the confidence they gain to learn independently, analyse problems critically and communicate effectively on their own.

difference.label: WHAT YOU RECEIVE
difference.title: Every Lesson Is Built Around The Student

item1.title: Personalised Learning Roadmap
item1.desc: A structured study plan designed around each student's current level and long-term goals.

item2.title: Progress Tracking
item2.desc: Learning progress is continuously monitored and lessons adapt as students improve.

item3.title: Vocabulary Development
item3.desc: Build useful vocabulary through practical usage rather than memorising isolated word lists.

item4.title: Writing Feedback
item4.desc: Detailed corrections explaining both what should change and why.

item5.title: Speaking Correction
item5.desc: Continuous coaching on pronunciation, fluency and natural expression.

item6.title: Recurring Mistake Analysis
item6.desc: Repeated errors are tracked and corrected before they become long-term habits.

item7.title: Homework Built Around Weaknesses
item7.desc: Assignments target each student's weakest areas instead of giving identical worksheets to everyone.

item8.title: Questions Between Lessons
item8.desc: Students are welcome to ask short questions outside class whenever they need clarification.

expectations.label: WHAT TO EXPECT
expectations.title: Every Lesson Has A Purpose
expectations.assessmentDesc: Every student begins with an assessment to identify their current level, strengths, weaknesses and learning goals. Lessons are then planned around those findings rather than following a generic syllabus.
expectations.structuredDesc: Every lesson has clear objectives. Students always know what they are learning, why it matters and how it connects to previous lessons.
expectations.feedbackDesc: Students receive corrections throughout lessons instead of waiting until the end. This encourages immediate improvement and helps prevent repeated mistakes.
expectations.growthDesc: The objective is never short-term memorisation. Lessons are designed to develop confidence, independence and lifelong English skills that continue beyond examinations.

belief.label: WHAT I BELIEVE
belief.title:
Great Teaching Builds
Independent Learners.
belief.quote:
"Learning should never rely on fear.

Mistakes are evidence that learning is happening.

Confidence comes from preparation.

Discipline creates freedom.

My role is to help students eventually no longer need me."

hero.label: STUDENT AND PARENT POLICIES
hero.title:
Clear Policies.
Better Learning.
hero.description: These policies are designed to create a professional, respectful and productive learning environment for every student and family.

policies.introduction:
By enrolling in lessons with English coach Yeo, students and parents acknowledge that they have read and agreed to the following policies.

These guidelines help ensure lessons remain organised, effective and fair for everyone.

enrollment.label: SECTION 01
enrollment.title: Enrollment & Payment
enrollment.paymentDesc: Tuition is calculated based on the number of scheduled lessons for the upcoming month. An invoice will be issued on the 25th of each month. Payment is required before the first scheduled lesson of the new month. If payment has not been received before the second scheduled lesson, all remaining lessons will be suspended until payment has been completed.
enrollment.confirmationDesc: Together with the monthly invoice, students or parents will be asked to confirm whether lessons will continue for the following month. This allows lesson schedules to be arranged in advance and ensures reserved lesson times remain available.
enrollment.withdrawalDesc: Students wishing to discontinue lessons are kindly requested to notify me before the 25th of the current month. As lesson schedules are planned and reserved on a monthly basis, advance notice allows sufficient time to reorganise the teaching schedule and offer the reserved monthly lesson plan to another student. If no notice is received before the monthly confirmation date, lessons will be assumed to discontinue into the following month.

schedule.label: SECTION 02
schedule.title: Scheduling Policies
schedule.reservedDesc: Each student's weekly lesson time is reserved exclusively for them. Consistent scheduling supports long-term learning progress and ensures fairness for all students. Reserved lesson times may be offered to students on the waiting list if lessons are discontinued.
schedule.durationDesc: Lesson lengths are designed according to each programme. Cambridge Young Learners, School English and Communication English lessons are 60 minutes. IELTS and SAT Reading & Writing lessons are 90 minutes.
schedule.rescheduleDesc: Students who need to reschedule should provide at least 24 hours' notice. Lessons cancelled with sufficient notice will be rescheduled based on mutual availability. Lessons cancelled less than one hour before the scheduled lesson may be considered completed unless the absence is due to an emergency. If I need to reschedule a lesson, a make-up lesson will always be arranged.
schedule.lateDesc: Lessons finish at their originally scheduled time. Students arriving late will still finish at the scheduled ending time, ensuring fairness for students with lessons afterwards.
schedule.holidayDesc: If a student plans to take a long break (such as holidays, examinations or summer programmes), please provide at least two weeks' notice. Depending on availability, lessons may be temporarily paused, rescheduled or the reserved lesson time released.

learning.label: SECTION 03
learning.title: Learning Expectations
learning.homeworkDesc: Progress depends not only on lesson time but also on consistent independent practice. Students are expected to complete assigned homework, review lesson notes, revise vocabulary regularly, and practise speaking and writing between lessons. Homework should normally be completed before the next lesson or within one week unless otherwise instructed.
learning.progressDesc: Every student follows an individual learning roadmap. Progress is continuously monitored across grammar, vocabulary, reading, writing, speaking, pronunciation and recurring mistakes. Lessons are adapted according to each student's progress rather than following a fixed textbook alone.
learning.assessmentDesc: Students may request a paid 30-minute diagnostic assessment before beginning long-term coaching. The assessment identifies current ability, strengths, weaknesses, learning goals and provides a recommended study roadmap.

communication.label: SECTION 04
communication.title: Communication
communication.hoursDesc: To maintain work-life balance and ensure adequate lesson preparation, messages are normally answered between 10:00 and 21:00 (Vietnam Time). Messages received outside these hours will be answered as soon as reasonably possible.
communication.questionsDesc: Students are encouraged to ask brief academic questions whenever clarification is needed. However, detailed explanations, essay corrections or solving multiple practice questions form part of scheduled coaching sessions and will be addressed during lessons.

professional.label: SECTION 05
professional.title: Professional Standards
professional.environmentDesc: Students should prepare all required materials, join lessons punctually and minimise distractions. Mutual respect and professional communication are expected from both coach and student throughout the learning journey.
professional.responsibilitiesDesc: The coach is committed to preparing lessons appropriate to each student's level, providing constructive feedback, monitoring progress continuously and maintaining a respectful, supportive and professional learning environment.

general.label: SECTION 06
general.title: General Policies
general.recordingDesc: Lessons may not be audio recorded, video recorded, distributed or shared without the prior consent of both the coach and the student.
general.integrityDesc: Coaching is designed to develop genuine English proficiency. Assistance intended to facilitate plagiarism, cheating or academic dishonesty will not be provided.
general.updatesDesc: These policies may be updated periodically to reflect improvements to teaching practices or administrative procedures. The latest version published on this website supersedes all previous versions.
